title: Refactor so each function head destructures only the fields it needs
prompt:
Given:

```
def greet(%{name: name, age: age, email: email, preferences: prefs, role: role}) do
  "Hi #{name}!"
end
```

This head destructures 5 fields but only uses `name`. Refactor to `def greet(%{name: name}), do: "Hi #{name}!"`. Then add a second head for when `name` is missing: `def greet(_), do: "Hi!"`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Greeter do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Explicit test of 'don't over-destructure' — complex-extractions anti-pattern. Sonnet often keeps the full match.
